text:
Do not fall in love with a property before the numbers work.

Every experienced investor has a story about the one they wanted too badly.

The neighborhood felt right. The seller was motivated. The pictures looked clean.

And then they ran the numbers.

ARV came in lower than expected. Rehab was deeper than the inspector caught. The margin disappeared before they made an offer.

The deal that survives the math is the deal worth chasing. The one that doesn't — save your time.

Know the numbers before you chase the deal.

👉 Save this. Try the Deal Analyzer before your next walkthrough — link in bio.

visual.openai_prompt: Create a premium real estate deal-room visual for The Real Estate Deal Lab. Dark navy background (#070F1C), refined gold accents (#E0A82E), serious investor education mood, clean editorial composition. Show a marked-up deal analysis sheet with a large red X drawn through a deal that did not pass inspection — rehab line over budget, margin wiped out. A pen rests on the page next to a calculator. Subtle pro forma document texture. A blurred Florida residential exterior is visible in the background. Leave clean lower-left negative space for composited headline type. The image should feel premium, practical, and grounded, not flashy. Avoid luxury flex imagery, mansions as status symbols, cash piles, guru seminar energy, fake urgency, rendered words, neon, sci-fi, lofi, anime, Afrofuturism, and Layer8Culture visual motifs.
visual.headline: THE DEAL HAS TO SURVIVE THE MATH
visual.subtext: Don't fall in love before the numbers work.

text:
Cap rate is one of the most misquoted numbers in real estate.

Investors throw it around to make a deal sound better than it is. Here's what it actually tells you — and what it doesn't.

Swipe through and save this for your next rental analysis.

👉 Try the Deal Analyzer — link in bio.

slides.openai_prompt: Create a premium real estate deal-room visual for The Real Estate Deal Lab. Dark navy background (#070F1C), gold accents (#E0A82E). Show a clean rental property analysis sheet spread open on a strategy desk, with a gold-tinted percentage figure visible as a focal element. Calculator, pen, and subtle pro forma texture. Editorial lighting, serious investor mood. Leave clean lower-center negative space for composited headline type. Avoid rendered words, luxury flex, cash piles, neon, sci-fi, lofi, anime, Afrofuturism, Layer8Culture motifs.
slides.headline: WHAT CAP RATE ACTUALLY MEANS
slides.subtext: And why a 6% in Florida is not a 6% in Cleveland.

slides.openai_prompt: Create a premium real estate deal-room visual for The Real Estate Deal Lab. Dark navy background, gold accents. Show a clean formula worksheet close-up: a handwritten-style calculation card with two rows of figures representing net operating income and property value. Subtle document grain. Leave lower-left space for composited text. Avoid rendered words, luxury flex, neon, sci-fi, lofi, anime, Afrofuturism, Layer8Culture motifs.
slides.headline: THE FORMULA: NOI ÷ PURCHASE PRICE
slides.subtext: Net Operating Income divided by what you paid. That's your cap rate.

slides.openai_prompt: Create a premium real estate deal-room visual for The Real Estate Deal Lab. Dark navy background, gold accents. Show a marked-up deal sheet with two columns being compared — a high-priced market vs a mid-market, suggesting the same percentage means different things in different contexts. Pen underlining a key figure. Subtle editorial grain. Leave lower-left space for composited text. Avoid rendered words, luxury flex, neon, sci-fi, lofi, anime, Afrofuturism, Layer8Culture motifs.
slides.headline: SAME NUMBER, DIFFERENT RISK
slides.subtext: Cap rate doesn't adjust for vacancy, local rent growth, or exit options.

slides.openai_prompt: Create a premium real estate deal-room visual for The Real Estate Deal Lab. Dark navy background, gold accents. Show a Florida residential exterior silhouette at dusk against a clean navy sky, with a subtle deal analysis overlay in the foreground — a scorecard with a cap rate field and cash-on-cash return field both visible as shapes. Leave lower-left space for composited text. Avoid rendered words, luxury flex, neon, sci-fi, lofi, anime, Afrofuturism, Layer8Culture motifs.
slides.headline: PAIR IT WITH CASH-ON-CASH
slides.subtext: Cap rate measures the asset. Cash-on-cash measures your money at work.

slides.openai_prompt: Create a premium real estate deal-room visual for The Real Estate Deal Lab. Dark navy background, gold accents. text:
Every week we take a real deal — or a real deal type — and break down the numbers publicly.

No fluff. No theory.

ARV. Rehab estimate. Max offer. Projected cash flow or flip margin. What works and what kills the deal.

Deal Breakdown Weekly exists because most investors never see what the math actually looks like before someone buys. We fix that.

If you have been waiting to feel confident enough to make an offer — this is where that confidence gets built.

👉 Watch the full Deal Breakdown — link in bio.

Warm editorial lighting. Subtle pro forma texture and gold accent bar at the bottom. Leave clean lower-left negative space for composited headline type. text:
Is your deal actually a deal?

Most investors find out after they've made the offer.

The Deal Analyzer gives you ARV, max offer, rehab buffer, and projected return in one place — before you commit to anything.

Free. No signup wall. Just the numbers.
